title: Customers With Consistent Increasing Orders
description: Find customers whose order amounts strictly increase with time and have no decreasing step.
sql_solution: SELECT customer_id FROM (SELECT customer_id, amount, LAG(amount) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date) prev_amount FROM orders) GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 AND SUM(CASE WHEN prev_amount IS NOT NULL AND amount <= prev_amount THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0;
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

progression_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")))

result = (
    orders.withColumn("prev_amount", lag("amount").over(progression_window))
    .groupBy("customer_id")
    .agg(
        count(lit(1)).alias("order_count"),
        sum(
            when(
                col("prev_amount").isNotNull() & (col("amount") <= col("prev_amount")),
                lit(1)
            ).otherwise(lit(0))
        ).alias("non_increasing_steps")
    )
    .filter((col("order_count") > 1) & (col("non_increasing_steps") == 0))
    .select("customer_id")
)
